sast_review.reason: The regex `^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$` has polynomial worst-case time complexity due to overlapping repetitions (`[^@]+` can match the same characters in multiple ways). An attacker can craft an email-like string with many characters that causes catastrophic backtracking, leading to denial of service. The regex is applied to user-supplied input (`email`) without any length or complexity limits.

raw.summary: The code meets functional requirements but has a ReDoS vulnerability (CWE-1333) in the email regex, which is a real security concern. The SAST finding is a true positive.
